findings.description: The Java action implementation code shown before the JSP example is omitted. The source provides: `BatchEntity batch = new BatchEntity(); batch.setStatus("03"); context.setRequestScopedVar("batch", batch);` This code establishes why `name="batch.status"` resolves correctly in the JSP tag, illustrating the action-to-view data binding pattern.
findings.source_evidence: Source section 共通属性 usage example: `// アクションの実装例 BatchEntity batch = new BatchEntity(); batch.setStatus("03"); // "03"を設定 context.setRequestScopedVar("batch", batch);`

findings.description: The same Java action implementation code (`BatchEntity` setup with `setRequestScopedVar`) that appears before the codeタグ JSP examples in the source is omitted from s7. Without it, the JSP examples lack context showing how the request scope variable `batch` is populated.
findings.source_evidence: Source section codeタグ: `// アクションの実装例 BatchEntity batch = new BatchEntity(); batch.setStatus("03"); // "03"を設定 context.setRequestScopedVar("batch", batch);` appears before the code tag JSP examples.

findings.description: The knowledge file's CODE_NAME table omits two columns (SORT_ORDER and LANG) and all 9 English-language rows present in the source. SORT_ORDER determines item display order; LANG demonstrates multilingual support. The source table has 7 columns (ID, VALUE, SORT_ORDER, LANG, NAME, SHORT_NAME, OPTION01) and 14 rows (7 Japanese + 7 English). The knowledge file shows only 5 columns and 7 rows.
findings.source_evidence: Source CODE_NAME table header: `ID | VALUE | SORT_ORDER | LANG | NAME | SHORT_NAME | OPTION01` with rows for both `ja` and `en` languages (e.g., `0001 | 01 | 2 | en | Male | M | 0001-01-en`).
